description_long: This sculpture depicts a mythological sphinx, combining the body of a crouching lion with large, outstretched wings and the head of a youthful human. The figure is rendered in a classical style, with detailed curls in the hair and a serene, expressionless face. The wings are broad and textured, extending gracefully from the back and curving slightly outward. The lion's body is muscular, with defined paws and a curled tail resting on a rectangular plinth. The entire piece appears to be carved from a single block of pale stone, giving it a smooth, matte finish. The sculpture exudes a sense of strength and mystery, typical of ancient mythological guardians.
description: A pale stone sphinx sculpture with a human head, lion's body, and large, detailed wings, seated on a rectangular base. The piece is classical in style, with smooth surfaces and intricate hair and wing details.
description_view_0: This view highlights the left side of the sphinx, showing the full expanse of the left wing, the curled tail, and the detailed human face.
description_view_1: The front view emphasizes the human head, chest, and both forepaws, with the wings partially visible behind the figure.
description_view_2: The rear-left view displays the back of the head, both wings, and the muscular haunches, with the tail curling around the base.
description_view_3: This angle shows the right side and back of the sculpture, focusing on the right wing's curvature and the back of the head.
attribute_subject_matter: mythological sphinx with human head and lion body
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture is isolated on a plain background.
